title: crupina vulgaris
intro: Crupina vulgaris, also known as Cretan thistle or prickly thistle, is a biennial or perennial plant species in the sunflower family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Crupina vulgaris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a popular herbal remedy in some cultures.
